 43→public class ChainWrappingRule :
 44→    StandardRule(
 45→        id = "chain-wrapping",
 46→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 47→            setOf(
 48→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 49→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 50→            ),
 51→    ) {
 52→    private val sameLineTokens = TokenSet.create(MUL, DIV, PERC, ANDAND, OROR)
 53→    private val prefixTokens = TokenSet.create(PLUS, MINUS)
 54→    private val nextLineTokens = TokenSet.create(DOT, SAFE_ACCESS, ELVIS)
 55→    private val noSpaceAroundTokens = TokenSet.create(DOT, SAFE_ACCESS)
 56→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 57→
 58→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 59→        indentConfig =
 60→            IndentConfig(
 61→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 62→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 63→            )
 64→    }
 65→
 66→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 67→        node: ASTNode,
 68→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 69→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 70→    ) {
 71→        /*
 72→           org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement (DOT) | "."
 73→           org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl (WHITE_SPACE) | "\n        "
 74→           org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtCallExpression (CALL_EXPRESSION)
 75→         */
 76→        val elementType = node.elementType
 77→        if (nextLineTokens.contains(elementType)) {
 78→            if (node.isPartOfComment()) {
 79→                return
 80→            }
 81→            val nextLeaf = node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf()
 82→            if (nextLeaf.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() && !node.isElvisOperatorAndComment()) {
 83→                emit(node.startOffset, "Line must not end with \"${node.text}\"", true)
 84→                if (autoCorrect) {
 85→                    // rewriting
 86→                    // <prevLeaf><node="."><nextLeaf="\n"> to
 87→                    // <prevLeaf><delete space if any><nextLeaf="\n"><node="."><space if needed>
 88→                    // (or)
 89→                    // <prevLeaf><node="."><spaceBeforeComment><comment><nextLeaf="\n"> to
 90→                    // <prevLeaf><delete space if any><spaceBeforeComment><comment><nextLeaf="\n"><node="."><space if needed>
 91→                    if (node.elementType == ELVIS) {
 92→                        node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(node))
 93→                        node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
 94→                    } else {
 95→                        node.treeParent.removeChild(node)
 96→                        (nextLeaf as LeafElement).rawInsertAfterMe(node as LeafElement)
 97→                    }
 98→                }
 99→            }
100→        } else if (sameLineTokens.contains(elementType) || prefixTokens.contains(elementType)) {
101→            if (node.isPartOfComment()) {
102→                return
103→            }
104→            val prevLeaf = node.prevLeaf()
105→            if (
106→                prevLeaf?.elementType == WHITE_SPACE &&
107→                prevLeaf.textContains('\n') &&
108→                // fn(*typedArray<...>()) case
109→                (elementType != MUL || !prevLeaf.isPartOfSpread()) &&
110→                // unary +/-
111→                (!prefixTokens.contains(elementType) || !node.isInPrefixPosition())
112→            ) {
113→                emit(node.startOffset, "Line must not begin with \"${node.text}\"", true)
114→                if (autoCorrect) {
115→                    // rewriting
116→                    // <insertionPoint><prevLeaf="\n"><node="&&"><nextLeaf=" "> to
117→                    // <insertionPoint><prevLeaf=" "><node="&&"><nextLeaf="\n"><delete node="&&"><delete nextLeaf=" ">
118→                    // (or)
119→                    // <insertionPoint><spaceBeforeComment><comment><prevLeaf="\n"><node="&&"><nextLeaf=" "> to
120→                    // <insertionPoint><space if needed><node="&&"><spaceBeforeComment><comment><prevLeaf="\n"><delete node="&&"><delete nextLeaf=" ">
121→                    val nextLeaf = node.nextLeaf()
122→                    if (nextLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace) {
123→                        nextLeaf.node.treeParent.removeChild(nextLeaf.node)
124→                    }
125→                    val insertionPoint = prevLeaf.prevCodeLeaf() as LeafPsiElement
126→                    (node as LeafPsiElement).treeParent.removeChild(node)
127→                    insertionPoint.rawInsertAfterMe(node)
128→                    (insertionPoint as ASTNode).upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
129→                }
130→            }
131→        }
132→    }
133→
134→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfSpread() =
135→        prevCodeLeaf()?.let { leaf ->
136→            val type = leaf.elementType
137→            type == LPAR ||
138→                type == COMMA ||
139→                type == LBRACE ||
140→                type == ELSE_KEYWORD ||
141→                KtTokens.OPERATIONS.contains(type)
142→        } == true
143→
144→    private fun ASTNode.isInPrefixPosition() = treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == PREFIX_EXPRESSION
145→
146→    private fun ASTNode.isElvisOperatorAndComment(): Boolean {
147→        return elementType == ELVIS &&
148→            leaves().takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.isPartOfComment() }.any()
149→    }
150→}

 46→/**
 47→ * This rule wraps each multiline expression to a newline.
 48→ */
 49→public class MultilineExpressionWrapping :
 50→    StandardRule(
 51→        id = "multiline-expression-wrapping",
 52→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 53→            setOf(
 54→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 55→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 56→            ),
 57→    ),
 58→    Rule.Experimental,
 59→    Rule.OfficialCodeStyle {
 60→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 61→
 62→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 63→        indentConfig =
 64→            IndentConfig(
 65→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 66→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 67→            )
 68→    }
 69→
 70→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 71→        node: ASTNode,
 72→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 73→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 74→    ) {
 75→        if (node.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION &&
 76→            (node.treeParent.elementType !in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION || node.isRightHandSideOfBinaryExpression())
 77→        ) {
 78→            visitExpression(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 79→        }
 80→        if (node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION &&
 81→            node.treeParent.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION
 82→        ) {
 83→            visitExpression(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 84→        }
 85→    }
 86→
 87→    private fun visitExpression(
 88→        node: ASTNode,
 89→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 90→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 91→    ) {
 92→        if (node.containsWhitespaceWithNewline() && node.needToWrapMultilineExpression()) {
 93→            node
 94→                .prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() }
 95→                .let { prevLeaf ->
 96→                    if (prevLeaf != null && !prevLeaf.textContains('\n')) {
 97→                        emit(node.startOffset, "A multiline expression should start on a new line", true)
 98→                        if (autoCorrect) {
 99→                            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.siblingIndentOf(node))
100→                            node
101→                                .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
102→                                .nextLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() && !it.isPartOfComment() && it.elementType != COMMA }
103→                                ?.takeIf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
104→                                ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.siblingIndentOf(node))
105→                        }
106→                    }
107→                }
108→        }
109→    }
110→
111→    private fun ASTNode.containsWhitespaceWithNewline(): Boolean {
112→        val lastLeaf = lastChildLeafOrSelf()
113→        return firstChildLeafOrSelf()
114→            .leavesIncludingSelf()
115→            .takeWhile { it != lastLeaf }
116→            .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || it.isRegularStringPartWithNewline() }
117→    }
118→
119→    private fun ASTNode.isRegularStringPartWithNewline() =
120→        elementType == ElementType.REGULAR_STRING_PART &&
121→            text.startsWith("\n")
122→
123→    private fun ASTNode.needToWrapMultilineExpression() =
124→        isValueInAnAssignment() ||
125→            isLambdaExpression() ||
126→            isValueArgument() ||
127→            isAfterArrow()
128→
129→    private fun ASTNode.isValueInAnAssignment() =
130→        null !=
131→            prevCodeSibling()
132→                ?.takeIf { it.elementType == EQ || it.elementType == OPERATION_REFERENCE }
133→                ?.takeUnless { it.isElvisOperator() }
134→                ?.takeUnless {
135→                    it.closingParenthesisOfFunctionOrNull()
136→                        ?.prevLeaf()
137→                        .isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
138→                }
139→
140→    private fun ASTNode?.isElvisOperator() =
141→        this != null &&
142→            elementType == OPERATION_REFERENCE &&
143→            firstChildNode.elementType == ElementType.ELVIS
144→
145→    private fun ASTNode.closingParenthesisOfFunctionOrNull() =
146→        takeIf { treeParent.elementType == FUN }
147→            ?.prevCodeLeaf()
148→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == RPAR }
149→
150→    private fun ASTNode.isLambdaExpression() =
151→        null !=
152→            treeParent
153→                .takeIf {
154→                    // Function literals in lambda expression have an implicit block (no LBRACE and RBRACE). So only wrap when the node is
155→                    // the first node in the block
156→                    it.elementType == BLOCK && it.firstChildNode == this
157→                }?.treeParent
158→                ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.FUNCTION_LITERAL }
159→                ?.treeParent
160→                ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.LAMBDA_EXPRESSION }
161→
162→    private fun ASTNode.isValueArgument() = treeParent.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT
163→
164→    private fun ASTNode.isAfterArrow() = prevCodeLeaf()?.elementType == ARROW
165→
166→    private fun ASTNode.isRightHandSideOfBinaryExpression() =
167→        null !=
168→            takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION }
169→                .takeIf { it?.prevCodeSibling()?.elementType == OPERATION_REFERENCE }
170→
171→    private companion object {
172→        // Based  on https://kotlinlang.org/spec/expressions.html#expressions
173→        val CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION =
174→            setOf(
175→                ARRAY_ACCESS_EXPRESSION,
176→                BINARY_WITH_TYPE,
177→                CALL_EXPRESSION,
178→                DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION,
179→                IF,
180→                IS_EXPRESSION,
181→                OBJECT_LITERAL,
182→                PREFIX_EXPRESSION,
183→                REFERENCE_EXPRESSION,
184→                SAFE_ACCESS_EXPRESSION,
185→                TRY,
186→                WHEN,
187→            )
188→    }
189→}

 10→public data class IndentConfig(
 11→    val indentStyle: IndentStyle,
 12→    /**
 13→     * The number of spaces that is equivalent to one tab
 14→     */
 15→    val tabWidth: Int,
 16→) {
 17→    /**
 18→     * To use the [IndentConfig] in a rule, the following needs to be done:
 19→     * 1. Implement interface [UsesEditorConfigProperties] on the rule
 20→     * 2. Register the used or properties
 21→     *    ```
 22→     *    override val editorConfigProperties: List<UsesEditorConfigProperties.EditorConfigProperty<*>> =
 23→     *        listOf(indentSizeProperty, indentStyleProperty)
 24→     *    ```
 25→     * 3. Initialize the IndentConfig
 26→     *    ```
 27→     *    indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 28→     *        indentStyle = node.getEditorConfigValue(indentStyleProperty),
 29→     *        tabWidth = node.getEditorConfigValue(indentSizeProperty)
 30→     *    )
 31→     *    ```
 32→     */
 33→    public constructor(
 34→        indentStyle: PropertyType.IndentStyleValue,
 35→        /**
 36→         * The number of spaces that is equivalent to one tab
 37→         */
 38→        tabWidth: Int,
 39→    ) : this(
 40→        indentStyle =
 41→            when (indentStyle) {
 42→                PropertyType.IndentStyleValue.tab -> TAB
 43→                PropertyType.IndentStyleValue.space -> SPACE
 44→            },
 45→        tabWidth = tabWidth,
 46→    )
 47→
 48→    public enum class IndentStyle { SPACE, TAB }
 49→
 50→    private val indentChar =
 51→        when (indentStyle) {
 52→            TAB -> '\t'
 53→            SPACE -> ' '
 54→        }
 55→
 56→    private val unexpectedIndentChar =
 57→        when (indentStyle) {
 58→            TAB -> ' '
 59→            SPACE -> '\t'
 60→        }
 61→
 62→    /**
 63→     * When disabled, rules may not enforce any indentation related changes regardless of other settings in this
 64→     * configuration.
 65→     */
 66→    public val disabled: Boolean
 67→        get() = tabWidth <= 0
 68→
 69→    /**
 70→     * Normalized indent of 1 level deep. Representation is either in spaces or a single tab depending on the
 71→     * configuration.
 72→     */
 73→    public val indent: String =
 74→        if (disabled) {
 75→            ""
 76→        } else {
 77→            when (indentStyle) {
 78→                TAB -> indentChar.toString()
 79→                SPACE -> indentChar.toString().repeat(tabWidth)
 80→            }
 81→        }
 82→
 83→    /**
 84→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the next indent level compared to the given node.
 85→     */
 86→    public fun childIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = node.indent().plus(indent)
 87→
 88→    /**
 89→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the same indent level as the given node.
 90→     */
 91→    public fun siblingIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = parentIndentOf(node).plus(indent)
 92→
 93→    /**
 94→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the same indent level as the parent of the given node.
 95→     */
 96→    public fun parentIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = node.treeParent.indent()
 97→
 98→    /**
 99→     * Converts [text] to a normalized indent. If [text] contains a new line, then only text after the last new line
100→     * is converted. This text may only contain spaces and tabs.
101→     *
102→     * Tabs in the given text are converted to spaces depending on the tab size of the indent style.
103→     *
104→     * When converting a text (possibly contains both tabs and spaces) to tabs, all tabs are first converted to
105→     * spaces. Each consecutive set of spaces is converted to tabs. Sapces which are left, e.g. not enough space to
106→     * complete a full tab, are ignored silently.
107→     */
108→    public fun toNormalizedIndent(text: String): String {
109→        val indent = getTextAfterLastNewLine(text)
110→        require(indent.matches(TABS_AND_SPACES))
111→        return when (indentStyle) {
112→            SPACE -> indent.replaceTabWithSpaces()
113→            TAB -> {
114→                "\t".repeat(indentLevelFrom(indent))
115→                // Silently swallow spaces if not enough spaces present to convert to a tab
116→                // val spaceCount = asSpaces.length - (tabCount * tabWidth)
117→                // "\t".repeat(tabCount) + " ".repeat(spaceCount)
118→            }
119→        }
120→    }
121→
122→    private fun getTextAfterLastNewLine(text: String): String {
123→        val index = text.indexOfLast { it == '\n' }
124→        val indent =
125→            if (index == -1) {
126→                text
127→            } else {
128→                text.substring(index + 1, text.length)
129→            }
130→        return indent
131→    }
132→
133→    private fun String.replaceTabWithSpaces() = replace("\t", " ".repeat(tabWidth))
134→
135→    /**
136→     * Gets the indent level for given [text]. If [text] contains a new line, then only text after the last new line
137→     * is considered. This text may only contain spaces and tabs.
138→     *
139→     * Tabs in the [text] are first converted to spaces depending on the tab size of the indent style. For each set
140→     * of spaces that is equivalent to one tab is counted as indentation level. Space that are remaining because
141→     * they do not fill up an entire tab, are ignored silently.
142→     */
143→    public fun indentLevelFrom(text: String): Int {
144→        val indent = getTextAfterLastNewLine(text)
145→        require(indent.matches(TABS_AND_SPACES))
146→        return indent.replaceTabWithSpaces().length / tabWidth
147→    }
148→
149→    public fun containsUnexpectedIndentChar(indentText: String): Boolean = indentText.contains(unexpectedIndentChar)
150→
151→    public fun indexOfFirstUnexpectedIndentChar(indentText: String): Int = indentText.indexOfFirst { it == unexpectedIndentChar }
152→
153→    public val unexpectedIndentCharDescription: String =
154→        when (indentStyle) {
155→            SPACE -> "tab"
156→            TAB -> "space"
157→        }
158→
159→    public companion object {
160→        private val TABS_AND_SPACES = Regex("[ \t]*")
161→
162→        public val DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG: IndentConfig =
163→            IndentConfig(
164→                indentStyle = INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue,
165→                tabWidth = INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY.defaultValue,
166→            )
167→    }
168→}

 34→/**
 35→ * Wraps a binary expression whenever the expression does not fit on the line. Wrapping a binary expression should take precedence before
 36→ * argument of function calls inside that binary expression are wrapped.
 37→ */
 38→public class BinaryExpressionWrappingRule :
 39→    StandardRule(
 40→        id = "binary-expression-wrapping",
 41→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 42→            setOf(
 43→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 44→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 45→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 46→            ),
 47→        visitorModifiers =
 48→            setOf(
 49→                RunAfterRule(ARGUMENT_LIST_WRAPPING_RULE_ID, REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED),
 50→            ),
 51→    ),
 52→    Rule.Experimental {
 53→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 54→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 55→
 56→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 57→        indentConfig =
 58→            IndentConfig(
 59→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 60→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 61→            )
 62→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 63→    }
 64→
 65→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 66→        node: ASTNode,
 67→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 68→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 69→    ) {
 70→        when (node.elementType) {
 71→            BINARY_EXPRESSION -> visitExpression(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 72→        }
 73→    }
 74→
 75→    private fun visitExpression(
 76→        node: ASTNode,
 77→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 78→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 79→    ) {
 80→        require(node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION)
 81→
 82→        // First check whether the entire expression has to be pushed to the next line after and assignment in a property or function
 83→        node
 84→            .takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType.anyOf(PROPERTY, FUN) }
 85→            ?.takeIf { binaryExpression ->
 86→                binaryExpression
 87→                    .prevSibling { it.elementType == EQ }
 88→                    ?.let { noNewLineInClosedRange(it, binaryExpression.firstChildLeafOrSelf()) }
 89→                    ?: false
 90→            }?.takeIf { it.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() }
 91→            ?.let { expression ->
 92→                emit(
 93→                    expression.startOffset,
 94→                    "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line between assignment and expression",
 95→                    true,
 96→                )
 97→                if (autoCorrect) {
 98→                    expression.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expression.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
 99→                }
100→            }
101→
102→        node
103→            .findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)
104→            ?.let { operationReference -> visitOperationReference(operationReference, emit, autoCorrect) }
105→    }
106→
107→    private fun visitOperationReference(
108→        node: ASTNode,
109→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
110→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
111→    ) {
112→        node
113→            .takeIf { it.elementType == OPERATION_REFERENCE }
114→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION }
115→            ?.takeIf { binaryExpression ->
116→                // Ignore binary expression inside raw string literals. Raw string literals are allowed to exceed max-line-length. Wrapping
117→                // (each) binary expression inside such a literal seems to create more chaos than it resolves.
118→                binaryExpression.parent { it.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY } == null
119→            }?.takeIf { it.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() }
120→            ?.let { operationReference ->
121→                if (node.isCallExpressionFollowedByLambdaArgument() || cannotBeWrappedAtOperationReference(operationReference)) {
122→                    // Wrapping after operation reference might not be the best place in case of a call expression or just won't work as
123→                    // the left hand side still does not fit on a single line
124→                    emit(operationReference.startOffset, "Line is exceeding max line length", false)
125→                } else {
126→                    operationReference
127→                        .nextSibling()
128→                        ?.let { nextSibling ->
129→                            emit(
130→                                nextSibling.startOffset,
131→                                "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line after operator in binary expression",
132→                                true,
133→                            )
134→                            if (autoCorrect) {
135→                                nextSibling.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(operationReference.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
136→                            }
137→                        }
138→                }
139→            }
140→    }
141→
142→    private fun ASTNode.isCallExpressionFollowedByLambdaArgument() =
143→        parent { it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST }
144→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION }
145→            ?.nextCodeSibling()
146→            .let { it?.elementType == LAMBDA_ARGUMENT }
147→
148→    private fun cannotBeWrappedAtOperationReference(operationReference: ASTNode) =
149→        operationReference
150→            .takeUnless { it.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION }
151→            ?.let {
152→                val stopAtOperationReferenceLeaf = operationReference.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
153→                maxLineLength <=
154→                    it
155→                        .leavesOnLine()
156→                        .takeWhile { leaf -> leaf != stopAtOperationReferenceLeaf }
157→                        .lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix()
158→            }
159→            ?: false
160→
161→    private fun ASTNode.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() = leavesOnLine().lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix() > maxLineLength
162→
163→    private fun Sequence<ASTNode>.lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix() =
164→        joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
165→            .dropWhile { it == '\n' }
166→            .length
167→}
168→
169→private fun IElementType.anyOf(vararg elementType: IElementType): Boolean = elementType.contains(this)
170→
171→public val BINARY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = BinaryExpressionWrappingRule().ruleId

 32→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 33→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 34→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 35→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.IElementType
 36→
 37→/**
 38→ * Wraps a binary expression whenever the expression does not fit on the line. Wrapping a binary expression should take precedence before
 39→ * argument of function calls inside that binary expression are wrapped.
 40→ */
 41→public class BinaryExpressionWrappingRule :
 42→    StandardRule(
 43→        id = "binary-expression-wrapping",
 44→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 45→            setOf(
 46→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 47→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 48→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 49→            ),
 50→        visitorModifiers =
 51→            setOf(
 52→                RunAfterRule(ARGUMENT_LIST_WRAPPING_RULE_ID, REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED),
 53→            ),
 54→    ),
 55→    Rule.Experimental {
 56→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 57→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 58→
 59→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 60→        indentConfig =
 61→            IndentConfig(
 62→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 63→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 64→            )
 65→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 66→    }
 67→
 68→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 69→        node: ASTNode,
 70→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 71→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 72→    ) {
 73→        when (node.elementType) {
 74→            BINARY_EXPRESSION -> visitExpression(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 75→        }
 76→    }
 77→
 78→    private fun visitExpression(
 79→        node: ASTNode,
 80→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 81→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 82→    ) {
 83→        require(node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION)
 84→
 85→        // First check whether the entire expression has to be pushed to the next line after and assignment in a property or function
 86→        node
 87→            .takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType.anyOf(PROPERTY, FUN) }
 88→            ?.takeIf { binaryExpression ->
 89→                binaryExpression
 90→                    .prevSibling { it.elementType == EQ }
 91→                    ?.let { noNewLineInClosedRange(it, binaryExpression.firstChildLeafOrSelf()) }
 92→                    ?: false
 93→            }?.takeIf { it.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() }
 94→            ?.let { expression ->
 95→                emit(
 96→                    expression.startOffset,
 97→                    "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line between assignment and expression",
 98→                    true,
 99→                )
100→                if (autoCorrect) {
101→                    expression.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expression.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
102→                }
103→            }
104→
105→        node
106→            .findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)
107→            ?.let { operationReference -> visitOperationReference(operationReference, emit, autoCorrect) }
108→    }
109→
110→    private fun visitOperationReference(
111→        node: ASTNode,
112→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
113→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
114→    ) {
115→        node
116→            .takeIf { it.elementType == OPERATION_REFERENCE }
117→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION }
118→            ?.takeIf { binaryExpression ->
119→                // Ignore binary expression inside raw string literals. Raw string literals are allowed to exceed max-line-length. Wrapping
120→                // (each) binary expression inside such a literal seems to create more chaos than it resolves.
121→                binaryExpression.parent { it.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY } == null
122→            }?.takeIf { it.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() }
123→            ?.let { operationReference ->
124→                if (node.isCallExpressionFollowedByLambdaArgument() || cannotBeWrappedAtOperationReference(operationReference)) {
125→                    // Wrapping after operation reference might not be the best place in case of a call expression or just won't work as
126→                    // the left hand side still does not fit on a single line
127→                    emit(operationReference.startOffset, "Line is exceeding max line length", false)
128→                } else {
129→                    operationReference
130→                        .nextSibling()
131→                        ?.let { nextSibling ->
132→                            emit(
133→                                nextSibling.startOffset,
134→                                "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line after operator in binary expression",
135→                                true,
136→                            )
137→                            if (autoCorrect) {
138→                                nextSibling.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(operationReference.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
139→                            }
140→                        }
141→                }
142→            }
143→    }
144→
145→    private fun ASTNode.isCallExpressionFollowedByLambdaArgument() =
146→        parent { it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST }
147→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION }
148→            ?.nextCodeSibling()
149→            .let { it?.elementType == LAMBDA_ARGUMENT }
150→
151→    private fun cannotBeWrappedAtOperationReference(operationReference: ASTNode) =
152→        operationReference
153→            .takeUnless { it.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION }
154→            ?.let {
155→                val stopAtOperationReferenceLeaf = operationReference.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
156→                maxLineLength <=
157→                    it
158→                        .leavesOnLine()
159→                        .takeWhile { leaf -> leaf != stopAtOperationReferenceLeaf }
160→                        .lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix()
161→            }
162→            ?: false ||
163→            exceedsMaxLineLengthAfterWrappingOnElvisOperator(operationReference)
164→
165→    private fun exceedsMaxLineLengthAfterWrappingOnElvisOperator(operationReference: ASTNode): Boolean {
166→        // The ChainWrappingRule never allows the elvis operator to be wrapped at the end of a line. Wrapping after the elvis operator only
167→        // makes sense when it is certain that the result will be valid according to the ChainWrappingRule. In case the right-hand side of
168→        // the elvis operator is still exceeding the max-line-length when it is preceded by the elvis operator, then wrapping should not be
169→        // autocorrected as it results in a conflict with ChainWrappingRule that can not be resolved.
170→        if (operationReference.firstChildLeafOrSelf().elementType != ELVIS) {
171→            return false
172→        }
173→        val rightHandSide = operationReference.nextCodeSibling() ?: return false
174→        val rightHandSideLength =
175→            rightHandSide
176→                .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
177→                .leavesIncludingSelf()
178→                .takeWhile { it.prevLeafOnSameLineOrNull() != null }
179→                .sumOf { it.text.length }
180→        // When wrapped:
181→        //     val foobar = leftHandSize
182→        //         ?: rightHandSide
183→        // The length of the line containing the right hand side is calculated as
184→        //     length of indent + "?: ".length + rightHandSideLength
185→        val lengthAfterWrapping =
186→            indentConfig.childIndentOf(operationReference).length +
187→                "?: ".length +
188→                rightHandSideLength
189→        return lengthAfterWrapping > maxLineLength
190→    }
191→
192→    private tailrec fun ASTNode.prevLeafOnSameLineOrNull(): ASTNode? =
193→        prevSibling()
194→            ?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
195→            ?.let { prevLeaf ->
196→                if (prevLeaf.text.contains('\n')) {
197→                    null
198→                } else {
199→                    prevLeaf.prevLeafOnSameLineOrNull() ?: prevLeaf
200→                }
201→            }
202→
203→    private fun ASTNode.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() = leavesOnLine().lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix() > maxLineLength
204→
205→    private fun Sequence<ASTNode>.lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix() =
206→        joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
207→            .dropWhile { it == '\n' }
208→            .length
209→}
210→
211→private fun IElementType.anyOf(vararg elementType: IElementType): Boolean = elementType.contains(this)
212→
213→public val BINARY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = BinaryExpressionWrappingRule().ruleId

 51→/**
 52→ * AssertJ style assertion for verifying KtLint rules. This class is intended to be used as follows:
 53→ *
 54→ * ```
 55→ * // Create an assertThat assertion for a specific rule or set of rules. If possible define it at the class level
 56→ * val someRuleAssertThat = SomeRule().assertThat()
 57→ *
 58→ * @Test
 59→ * fun `Some test`() {
 60→ *     val code = .... // Original code to be formatted
 61→ *     val formattedCode = .... // The code as it should be formatted
 62→ *     someRuleAssertThat(code)
 63→ *         .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "some-error-description")
 64→ *         .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 65→ * }
 66→ * ```
 67→ */
 68→@Suppress("MemberVisibilityCanBePrivate")
 69→public class KtLintAssertThat(
 70→    /**
 71→     * Provider of a rule which is the subject of the test, e.g. the rule for which the AssertThat is created.
 72→     */
 73→    private val ruleProvider: RuleProvider,
 74→    /**
 75→     * The code which is to be linted and formatted.
 76→     */
 77→    private val code: String,
 78→    /**
 79→     * Providers of rules which have to be executed in addition to the main rule when linting/formatting the code. Note
 80→     * that lint errors for those rules are suppressed.
 81→     */
 82→    private val additionalRuleProviders: MutableSet<RuleProvider>,
 83→    /**
 84→     * EditorConfig properties to be applied by default when linting/formatting the code.
 85→     */
 86→    private val editorConfigProperties: MutableSet<Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>>,
 87→) {
 88→    private var filePath: String? = null
 89→    private var kotlinScript = false
 90→
 91→    /**
 92→     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] properties to be used by the rule. This function can be called multiple times.
 93→     * Properties which have been set before, are silently overwritten with the new vale.
 94→     */
 95→    public fun withEditorConfigOverride(vararg properties: Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>): KtLintAssertThat {
 96→        editorConfigProperties.addAll(properties)
 97→
 98→        return this
 99→    }

 34→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 35→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.IElementType
 36→
 37→/**
 38→ * Wraps a binary expression whenever the expression does not fit on the line. Wrapping a binary expression should take precedence before
 39→ * argument of function calls inside that binary expression are wrapped.
 40→ */
 41→public class BinaryExpressionWrappingRule :
 42→    StandardRule(
 43→        id = "binary-expression-wrapping",
 44→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 45→            setOf(
 46→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 47→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 48→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 49→            ),
 50→        visitorModifiers =
 51→            setOf(
 52→                RunAfterRule(ARGUMENT_LIST_WRAPPING_RULE_ID, REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED),
 53→            ),
 54→    ),
 55→    Rule.Experimental {
 56→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 57→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 58→
 59→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 60→        indentConfig =
 61→            IndentConfig(
 62→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 63→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 64→            )
 65→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 66→    }
 67→
 68→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 69→        node: ASTNode,
 70→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 71→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 72→    ) {
 73→        when (node.elementType) {
 74→            BINARY_EXPRESSION -> visitExpression(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 75→        }
 76→    }
 77→
 78→    private fun visitExpression(
 79→        node: ASTNode,
 80→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 81→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 82→    ) {
 83→        require(node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION)
 84→
 85→        // First check whether the entire expression has to be pushed to the next line after and assignment in a property or function
 86→        node
 87→            .takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType.anyOf(PROPERTY, FUN) }
 88→            ?.takeIf { binaryExpression ->
 89→                binaryExpression
 90→                    .prevSibling { it.elementType == EQ }
 91→                    ?.let { noNewLineInClosedRange(it, binaryExpression.firstChildLeafOrSelf()) }
 92→                    ?: false
 93→            }?.takeIf { it.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() }
 94→            ?.let { expression ->
 95→                emit(
 96→                    expression.startOffset,
 97→                    "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line between assignment and expression",
 98→                    true,
 99→                )
100→                if (autoCorrect) {
101→                    expression.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expression.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
102→                }
103→            }
104→
105→        node
106→            .findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)
107→            ?.let { operationReference -> visitOperationReference(operationReference, emit, autoCorrect) }
108→    }
109→
110→    private fun visitOperationReference(
111→        node: ASTNode,
112→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
113→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
114→    ) {
115→        node
116→            .takeIf { it.elementType == OPERATION_REFERENCE }
117→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION }
118→            ?.takeIf { binaryExpression ->
119→                // Ignore binary expression inside raw string literals. Raw string literals are allowed to exceed max-line-length. Wrapping
120→                // (each) binary expression inside such a literal seems to create more chaos than it resolves.
121→                binaryExpression.parent { it.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY } == null
122→            }?.takeIf { it.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() }
123→            ?.let { operationReference ->
124→                if (node.isCallExpressionFollowedByLambdaArgument() || cannotBeWrappedAtOperationReference(operationReference)) {
125→                    // Wrapping after operation reference might not be the best place in case of a call expression or just won't work as
126→                    // the left hand side still does not fit on a single line
127→                    emit(operationReference.startOffset, "Line is exceeding max line length", false)
128→                } else {
129→                    operationReference
130→                        .nextSibling()
131→                        ?.let { nextSibling ->
132→                            emit(
133→                                nextSibling.startOffset,
134→                                "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line after operator in binary expression",
135→                                true,
136→                            )
137→                            if (autoCorrect) {
138→                                nextSibling.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(operationReference.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
139→                            }
140→                        }
141→                }
142→            }
143→    }
144→
145→    private fun ASTNode.isCallExpressionFollowedByLambdaArgument() =
146→        parent { it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST }
147→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION }
148→            ?.nextCodeSibling()
149→            .let { it?.elementType == LAMBDA_ARGUMENT }
150→
151→    private fun cannotBeWrappedAtOperationReference(operationReference: ASTNode) =
152→        operationReference
153→            .takeUnless { it.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION }
154→            ?.let {
155→                val stopAtOperationReferenceLeaf = operationReference.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
156→                maxLineLength <=
157→                    it
158→                        .leavesOnLine()
159→                        .takeWhile { leaf -> leaf != stopAtOperationReferenceLeaf }
160→                        .lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix()
161→            }
162→            ?: false ||
163→            exceedsMaxLineLengthAfterWrappingOnElvisOperator(operationReference)
164→
165→    private fun exceedsMaxLineLengthAfterWrappingOnElvisOperator(operationReference: ASTNode): Boolean {
166→        // The ChainWrappingRule never allows the elvis operator to be wrapped at the end of a line. Wrapping after the elvis operator only
167→        // makes sense when it is certain that the result will be valid according to the ChainWrappingRule. In case the right-hand side of
168→        // the elvis operator is still exceeding the max-line-length when it is preceded by the elvis operator, then wrapping should not be
169→        // autocorrected as it results in a conflict with ChainWrappingRule that can not be resolved.
170→        if (operationReference.firstChildLeafOrSelf().elementType != ELVIS) {
171→            return false
172→        }
173→        val rightHandSide = operationReference.nextCodeSibling() ?: return false
174→        val rightHandSideFirstLineLength = rightHandSide.firstLineLength()
175→        // When wrapped:
176→        //     val foobar = leftHandSize
177→        //         ?: rightHandSide
178→        // The length of the line containing the right hand side is calculated as
179→        //     length of indent + "?: ".length + rightHandSideLength
180→        // Note: childIndentOf returns indent with newline prefix, so we need to subtract 1 for the newline character
181→        val lengthAfterWrapping =
182→            indentConfig.childIndentOf(operationReference).length - 1 +
183→                "?: ".length +
184→                rightHandSideFirstLineLength
185→        return lengthAfterWrapping > maxLineLength
186→    }
187→
188→    private fun ASTNode.firstLineLength(): Int {
189→        val stopAtLeaf = lastChildLeafOrSelf()
190→        return firstChildLeafOrSelf()
191→            .leavesIncludingSelf()
192→            .takeWhile { !it.textContains('\n') && it != stopAtLeaf }
193→            .sumOf { it.textLength }
194→            .plus(stopAtLeaf.text.substringBefore('\n').length)
195→    }
196→
197→    private fun ASTNode.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() = leavesOnLine().lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix() > maxLineLength
198→
199→    private fun Sequence<ASTNode>.lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix() =
200→        joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
201→            .dropWhile { it == '\n' }
202→            .length
203→}
204→
205→private fun IElementType.anyOf(vararg elementType: IElementType): Boolean = elementType.contains(this)
206→
207→public val BINARY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = BinaryExpressionWrappingRule().ruleId
208→

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY
 4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.KtLintAssertThat
 5→import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test
 6→
 7→/**
 8→ * Test for the issue: "Endless loop in wrapping binary expression after elvis operator"
 9→ * https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2296
10→ *
11→ * When a line ends with `?:` and the right-hand side expression is long enough that wrapping
12→ * after the elvis operator would still exceed max line length (because ChainWrappingRule will
13→ * move `?:` to the start of the next line), ktlint should not try to auto-correct as it
14→ * would result in an endless loop.
15→ */
16→class BinaryExpressionWrappingElvisIssueTest {
17→    private val binaryExpressionWrappingRuleAssertThat = KtLintAssertThat.assertThatRule { BinaryExpressionWrappingRule() }
18→
19→    @Test
20→    fun `Given a binary expression with elvis operator where right-hand side exceeds max line length when wrapped`() {
21→        // When the right-hand side is so long that even when placed on a new line with "?: " prefix,
22→        // it would still exceed max line length, wrapping should not be auto-corrected to avoid an
23→        // endless loop with ChainWrappingRule.
24→        // Line: "    foobar ?: throw Exception()" = 31 chars
25→        // After ChainWrappingRule: "        ?: throw Exception()" = 28 chars
26→        // With max_line_length = 27, wrapped line still exceeds, so auto-correct would fail.
27→        val code =
28→            """
29→            val foo =
30→                foobar ?: throw Exception()
31→            """.trimIndent()
32→        binaryExpressionWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
33→            .withEditorConfigOverride(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to 27)
34→            .hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(2, 12, "Line is exceeding max line length")
35→    }
36→
37→    @Test
38→    fun `Given a binary expression with elvis operator where right-hand side fits when wrapped`() {
39→        // When the right-hand side is short enough that it fits when placed on a new line
40→        // with "?: " prefix, wrapping should be auto-corrected.
41→        // Line: "    foobar ?: throw Exception()" = 31 chars
42→        // After ChainWrappingRule: "        ?: throw Exception()" = 28 chars
43→        // With max_line_length = 30, the original exceeds but wrapped fits.
44→        val code =
45→            """
46→            val foo =
47→                foobar ?: throw Exception()
48→            """.trimIndent()
49→        val formattedCode =
50→            """
51→            val foo =
52→                foobar ?:
53→                    throw Exception()
54→            """.trimIndent()
55→        binaryExpressionWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
56→            .withEditorConfigOverride(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to 30)
57→            .hasLintViolation(2, 14, "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line after operator in binary expression")
58→            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
59→    }
60→}
61→
